title: Prove Fp::inv() and Fp::div() via Fermat's little theorem in Lean4
description:
Prove that `Fp::inv()` correctly computes multiplicative inverse using Fermat's little theorem: `a^(P-2) mod P = a⁻¹ mod P` for nonzero `a`.

## What to prove
1. `inv(a)` returns `a^(P-2) mod P` — follows from `mod_pow_mont` correctness
2. `a * inv(a) ≡ 1 (mod P)` for `a ≠ 0` — follows from Fermat's little theorem (available in Mathlib as `ZMod.pow_card_sub_one_eq_one`)
3. `div(a, b) = a * inv(b)` — structural, follows from the implementation

## Implementation
- `inv()` calls `mod_pow_mont(self.value, P - 2)` (gfp/mod.rs)
- `div()` calls `self * other.inv()` (operator overload)

## Proof strategy
1. Use `mod_pow_mont` correctness (from dependency) to get `inv(a).val = a^(P-2) mod P`
2. Apply Mathlib's `ZMod.pow_card_sub_one_eq_one` or `ZMod.units_pow_card_sub_two_eq_one` to get `a^(P-2) * a ≡ 1 (mod P)`
3. Transfer through the `FpVal P ≃+* ZMod P.val` ring isomorphism (already in FpField.lean)

This completes the full Fp arithmetic chain: every operation (new, value, add, sub, mul, neg, inv, div, pow) will have a machine-checked correctness proof.

## Difficulty
Low-Medium — mostly connecting existing pieces. The Mathlib integration through the ring isomorphism is the main work.
